channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Standards for Universities to Combat Antisemitism and Racism
item.description: The Albanese government has introduced new mandatory standards requiring Australian universities to implement robust measures against racism and antisemitism, effective from Monday. Vice chancellors w...

item.title: US Congressman Ro Khanna alleges armed Israeli settlers detained his delegation during West Bank visit
item.description: US Congressman Ro Khanna says he and several other Americans were detained for about 90 minutes by armed Israeli settlers while visiting the occupied West Bank. According to Khanna, the settlers, carr...

item.title: Bank of Canada maintains interest rates amid economic stability and inflation control
item.description: The Bank of Canada is anticipated to keep interest rates unchanged at 2.25% for the sixth consecutive time, as lower oil prices and improved economic data ease previous policy tensions. Since February...

item.title: Iran and US escalate conflict over Strait of Hormuz control
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between Iran and the US over control of the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides launching strikes and accusing each other of aggression. Iran claims to have c...

item.title: El Niño's Impact: South Africa Faces Extreme Heat and Drought Risks
item.description: A powerful El Niño climate pattern is rapidly developing in the Pacific Ocean, with a 90% chance of persisting until November. This phenomenon will likely bring extreme heat, drought, and veld fires t...

item.title: Philippine Sports Commission plans to expand tennis infrastructure and competitions
item.description: The Philippine Sports Commission (PSC) is planning to increase the number of tennis courts and organize more high-level tournaments to ensure the continued growth of tennis in the country. This initia...

item.title: Successful arrest of Ansaru terrorists after Oyo schoolchildren rescue
item.description: A viral video has captured the moment security operatives arrested suspected Ansaru terrorists involved in the abduction of over 50 schoolchildren and teachers from Oyo State. The victims, held captiv...

item.title: Understanding Tolyamory: A Common Non-Monogamous Relationship Dynamic
item.description: The article explores the concept of tolyamory, a relationship dynamic where one or both partners tolerate their partner's non-monogamous behavior without explicit agreement. Coined by Dan Savage, this...

item.title: TOI News Desk: 24/7 Journalism for Global Readership
item.description: The Times of India's news desk operates as a relentless journalistic engine, ensuring global audiences receive real-time coverage of breaking news, in-depth analyses, and insightful reports. With an u...

item.title: HFI Initiative Aims to Standardize Power-On Experience for RISC-V Hardware
item.description: The Harmonic Firmware Initiative (HFI) is developing a standardized power-on firmware experience for RISC-V hardware, aiming to replicate the x86 world's POST-like initialization and BIOS setup utilit...

item.title: Former Qatar Leader Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Dies At 74, Shaped Nation's Growth
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, former leader of Qatar, passed away at 74. He led Qatar from 1995 to 2013, transforming the small emirate into a regional and global power. Under his leadership, Qat...

item.title: Kenya and Ethiopia Strengthen Cross-Border Electricity Agreement
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has signed a new electricity supply agreement with Ethiopia's state-owned utility, securing power at approximately KSh 20 per kWh. The deal, signed on July 10, ...

item.title: Former U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance Says Senate Scrutiny of Jeffrey Epstein Files Could Shape Todd Blanche's Confirmation
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is expected to face intense scrutiny from the Senate Judiciary Committee as lawmakers consider whether to confirm him permanently as the nation's attorney general....

item.title: Brendon McCullum steps down as England Test coach after four years
item.description: Brendon McCullum has been immediately removed from his role as England's Test cricket coach, marking the end of the controversial Bazball era. The ECB announced the decision on Sunday, citing a need t...

item.title: Mark Milke advocates for Canada's renewal through merit-based policies
item.description: Mark Milke, founder of the Aristotle Foundation for Public Policy, argues against Alberta's push for separation from Canada, calling it akin to starting a war. He emphasizes that the core issue in Con...

item.title: Stirling Proposed as Nuclear Site Amid Political Opposition
item.description: A report by Great British Energy Nuclear has suggested Stirling as a potential site for a new nuclear power plant, sparking immediate criticism from Scottish politicians. The proposal, part of a broad...

item.title: Canadian Arrested in Laguna Over Past Child Sex Offenses
item.description: The Philippine immigration authorities arrested a Canadian national, Orville Frank Mader, for overstaying his visa and alleged past child sex offenses. The 52-year-old was detained at Subic Bay in Zam...

item.title: The Enduring Legacy of Cricket in India: Why Fans Keep Coming Back
item.description: The article explores the deep-rooted cultural significance of cricket in India, highlighting how the sport continues to captivate fans despite the rise of other global sports. It references iconic pla...

item.title: Saudi Arabia supports dialysis for thousands of Sudanese amid healthcare crisis
item.description: More than 5,500 Sudanese patients across twelve states are receiving critical kidney dialysis treatment thanks to ongoing support from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief). Th...

item.title: Nigeria's Security Forces Successfully Rescue Abducted Pupils and Teachers, Restore Public Confidence
item.description: The Deputy Spokesperson of the House of Representatives, Philip Agbese, has praised President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and Nigeria's security agencies for successfully rescuing abducted pupils and teachers i...
